methods.description: Process for digesting samples using a microwave-assisted closed system to quantify mineral elements such as Ca, Cu, Fe, K, Mg, Mn, Na, P, Se, and Zn. This method ensures low residual organic matter and is validated by performance metrics such as recovery rates and relative error.

steps.description: Sample digestion using a microwave-assisted closed system allowed quantification of the mineral elements Ca, Cu, Fe, K, Mg, Mn, Na, P, Se and Zn in a single sample preparation.
steps.searchable_summary: digest samples using microwave-assisted closed system for mineral quantification

steps.description: The residual carbon content was 0.15 g kg -1, indicating low level of organic matter after sample digestion.
steps.searchable_summary: measure residual carbon content to assess organic matter level

steps.description: The method exhibited good performance, confirmed by r values close to 1, LOD and LOQ with appropriate sensitivity to determine the minerals and satisfactory CV at the detection levels achieved.
steps.searchable_summary: validate method performance using r values, LOD, LOQ, and CV

steps.description: The working standards solutions for the calibration and reagent blanks used for the estimation of the linearity ranges and limits of detection and quantification were subjected to the digestion procedure.
steps.searchable_summary: digest working standards and reagent blanks for calibration

steps.description: Recovery results in polen matrix were within the range of 70 to 106%, mostly close to 100%, indicating adequacy of the analytical method.
steps.searchable_summary: assess recovery results in pollen matrix for method adequacy

steps.description: The results obtained in the laboratory analytical control evaluation, using the oyster tissue NIST 1566a certified reference material, under the same preparation conditions for bee pollen samples, showed adequacy of the method.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate method adequacy using NIST 1566a reference material

steps.description: For all elements, relative error was below 8%.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate relative error for all elements
